sourceMovie.title: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance (2002) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lock Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance clicked for you, Lock Up (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Drive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance clicked for you, Drive (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Novocaine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance clicked for you, Novocaine (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Death Wish

faq.question: What should I watch after Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance?
faq.answer: Try Lock Up, Drive, Novocaine—each shares DNA with Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance?
faq.answer: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance (2002) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
